title: Extra Villager Professions - Pyrotechnician
description: New villager trade set that offers various items related to Fireworks and Explosions
body:
Adds a new set of trades for things related to Fireworks and Explosions - using new Villager Trade datapack features added in 26.1. Fully server-side, no installation on the client required.

## IMPORTANT:

Due to limitations of this Vanilla method this is not a true brand new profession with its own block and mechanics. What it does is replace Vanilla profession trades with those ones, but only under certain conditions. If you dont do it correctly you wont get every trade!

## HOW TO ACCESS THE TRADES:

Rename a Villager to "**evp_pyro**" using a Name Tag, then make them become Cleric by claiming a **Brewing Stand** block. It is **important** you do it in that order, otherwise Level 1 trades wont be replaced. **Existing trades wont be modified!**

<details>
<summary>List of trades</summary>

Level 1:
- Emerald -> Gunpowder
- Fire Charge -> Emerald

Level 2:
- Diamond -> Emerald
- Emerald -> Firework Star (composition depends on Villager biome type)

Level 3:
- Emerald -> Paper
- Feather -> Emerald

Level 4:
- Emerald -> Flint and Steel (Unbreaking II)
- Glowstone Dust -> Emerald
- Gold Nugget -> Emerald
  
Level 5:
- Emerald -> TNT

</details>

## OPTIONAL:

Mod version has Assets bundled in, but datapack version is barebones.

By default, it will keep the Vanilla Cleric texture and English name. You can use this optional resourcepack to make Pyrotechnicians visually unique and have their name translated to a supported language (see resourcepack page for a list): [EVP Assets - Pyrotechnician](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/evp-assets-pyrotechnician) (**_Requires [ETF](https://modrinth.com/mod/entitytexturefeatures)_**)

![Minecraft Villager in a full-body leather suit and an iron helmet with visor](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/31be97499fe44b9d01b7b880cb6a78b352a45fab.png)

## UPDATES

Unfortunately when updating any changes to the existing trades wont be applied. That means if trades are changed in any way you will need to get a new villager to see them.

If you dont want to kill your villagers, there are handy commands to reset them, as below:

## UNINSTALLING

If you wish to uninstall the datapack, run this command (operator priveleges required):

```
/function evp_pyro:uninstall
```

It will reset every loaded Pyrotechnician to its original state.

Otherwise, you can run this command for a global uninstallation:

```
/function evp:uninstall
```

It will reset every loaded EVP series villager to its original state.

There is also an admin-only debug command that fully resets nearest Pyrotechnician villager:

```
/function evp_pyro:reset_nearest
```

### Other EVP datapacks:
- [Miner](https://modrinth.com/datapack/evp-miner)
- [Seafarer](https://modrinth.com/datapack/evp-seafarer)
